name: Mexican Manicotti
recipeInstructions:
1. Brown the ground beef with the diced onion, red bell pepper, and green bell pepper. Drain excess fat.
2. Mix in the refried beans, crushed dried basil, and chili powder.
3. Fill the uncooked manicotti shells with the meat mixture and place them in an ungreased 9x13 inch baking dish. If there is any leftover meat mixture, spread it evenly over the shells.
4. Combine the tomato sauce and picante sauce; pour evenly over the shells, ensuring they are completely covered.
5. Cover the dish with vented plastic wrap and microwave on high for 15 minutes. Turn the dish once during cooking.
6. Continue microwaving uncovered on 80% power for an additional 20 minutes, turning the dish once more.
7. In a separate bowl, combine the sour cream, sliced green onion, and chopped ripe olives.
8. Spoon the mixture over the casserole in the baking dish.
9. Sprinkle the shredded Monterey jack cheese evenly on top.
10. Microwave uncovered on high for 2 to 3 minutes or until the cheese has melted.
11. This recipe serves 8 portions.
